test.name: Two assertions from the same informant are one evidence unit (Standard 4)
test.description: Assertions a_012 ('Born 1845, Pennsylvania') and a_013 ('Father: Thomas Flynn') were BOTH reported by James Brown, the son-in-law, on Patrick's 1908 death certificate. They bear on the open parentage question q_001. GPS Standard 4 (evidence independence) says related information items from the same informant form one unit that gets no more credibility than the strongest single item. The skill should flag that a_012 and a_013 are NOT two independent pieces of evidence — they are one evidence unit because they share an informant — and must not be treated as mutual corroboration of Thomas Flynn's parentage.

input.user_message: Review the evidence classification on a_012 and a_013 and record the evidence-independence concern (they share one informant) in their classification.

judge_context: Should flag that a_012 and a_013 share a single informant (James Brown, son-in-law) and therefore form ONE evidence unit under GPS Standard 4, not two independent corroborating items
Should state that the unit gets no more credibility than its strongest single item — two facts from one informant are not mutual corroboration of Thomas Flynn's parentage
Should keep both assertions classified as secondary / family_not_present (it is not re-deriving the values, only noting the shared-informant independence concern)
